com.hp.workpath.api.webservices

Class HttpRequest

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.webservices.HttpRequest

  • public class HttpRequest
extends java.lang.Object
    Class for accessing HttpRequest. This class provides methods for accessing the headers, method type, attributes and the request body.
    Since:
    API 6

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  HttpRequest.HeaderKey
      Keys for Request-Header fields

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      HttpRequest()
      Default constructor

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.Object getAttribute(java.lang.String key)
      Retrieves the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key.
      java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> getAttributes()
      Retrieves the map object of all attributes
      java.lang.String getBody()
      Method to get request body
      java.lang.String getHeader(HttpRequest.HeaderKey key)
      Gets header value from HttpRequest.HeaderKey
      java.lang.String getHeader(java.lang.String key)
      Gets header value from the custom key
      java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> getHeaders()
      Returns the map object of all headers
      java.lang.String getMethod()
      Getter for following 4 type of http method names GET - Used to request data from a specified resource. POST - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource. DELETE - Method deletes the specified resource. PUT - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.
      void setAttribute(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> attribute)
      Method to set attribute
      void setBody(java.lang.String body)
      Method to set request body
      void setHeader(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> header)
      Method to set header
      void setMethod(java.lang.String method)
      Setter for http method names like GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

    • Constructor Detail

      • HttpRequest

        public HttpRequest()
        Default constructor
        Since:
        API 6

    • Method Detail

      • getMethod

        public java.lang.String getMethod()

        Getter for following 4 type of http method names

        • GET - Used to request data from a specified resource.
        • POST - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.
        • DELETE - Method deletes the specified resource.
        • PUT - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.

        Returns:
        It will return the http callback method name. e.g: GET, POST, DELETE & PUT
        Since:
        API 6

      • setMethod

        public void setMethod(java.lang.String method)

        Setter for http method names like GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

        Parameters:
        method - Name of the method

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Instance variable assigned as empty for empty input
        • Instance variable assigned as NULL for null input
        • Expected input - GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

        Since:
        API 6

      • getAttribute

        public java.lang.Object getAttribute(java.lang.String key)

        Retrieves the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key.

        Parameters:
        key - The key whose associated value is to be returned

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Expected input - String value

        Returns:

        The value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key More formally, if this map contains a mapping from a key k to a value v such that (key==null ? k==null : key.equals(k)), then this method returns v; otherwise it returns null. (There can be at most one such mapping.) If this map permits null values, then a return value of null does not necessarily indicate that the map contains no mapping for the key; it's also possible that the map explicitly maps the key to null. The containsKey operation may be used to distinguish these two cases.

        Since:
        API 6

      • getAttributes

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> getAttributes()
        Retrieves the map object of all attributes
        Returns:
        attribute - Map of all attributes
        Since:
        API 6

      • getHeader

        public java.lang.String getHeader(java.lang.String key)

        Gets header value from the custom key

        Parameters:
        key - API header key - e.g: X-Auth, User-Agent, Accept-Encoding etc

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Instance variable assigned as empty for empty key
        • Instance variable assigned as NULL for null key
        • Expected input - String value

        Returns:

        Header - value for the API header passed

        • Return can be null if the value for header is null
        • Return can be empty if the value for header is empty

        Since:
        API 6

      • getHeader

        public java.lang.String getHeader(HttpRequest.HeaderKey key)

        Gets header value from HttpRequest.HeaderKey

        Parameters:
        key - API header key - String from HttpRequest.HeaderKey

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Cannot be empty since the value is from HttpRequest.HeaderKey
        • Cannot be null since the value is from HttpRequest.HeaderKey
        • Expected input - Value from HttpRequest.HeaderKey

        Returns:

        Header - value for the API header passed

        • Return can be null if the value for header is null
        • Return can be empty if the value for header is empty

        Since:
        API 6

      • getHeaders

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> getHeaders()

        Returns the map object of all headers

        Returns:

        header - Map of all headers

        Since:
        API 6

      • setHeader

        public void setHeader(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> header)

        Method to set header

        Parameters:
        header - Map of header key and value

        Sets header as a map of header key and header value pair

        Since:
        API 6

      • setAttribute

        public void setAttribute(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> attribute)

        Method to set attribute

        Parameters:
        attribute - Map of attribute key and value

        Sets header as a map of attribute key and attribute value pair

        Since:
        API 6

      • getBody

        public java.lang.String getBody()
        Method to get request body
        Returns:
        Body of the request
        • Return can be null if the body is null
        • Return can be null if the body is empty
        Since:
        API 6

      • setBody

        public void setBody(java.lang.String body)
        Method to set request body
        Parameters:
        body - String request body

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Instance variable assigned as empty string for empty body
        • Expected input - String value

        Since:
        API 6
